q: According to Newton's second law, an object that is accelerating must have a net force acting on it.
In the absence of centripetal force, an object would move along a circle.
The magnitude of centripetal force can be expressed as $F_c = m r \omega^2$.
The tension in a string provides the required centripetal force for a ball to move in a circular path.
The electric force acting on an electron orbiting the nucleus is an example of centripetal force.
Frictional force behaves as a centripetal force when a car turns in a circular path on a flat road.
Banking of roads involves raising the inner edge of the curved road higher than the outer edge.
The angle that the inclined track makes with the horizontal is called the angle of banking.
The normal force $F_N$ exerted by a banked road is perpendicular to the road.
For every banked curve, there is a limiting speed at which friction between tires and road is not required.
The horizontal component of the normal force, $F_N \sin \theta$, acts towards the centre of the curve.
The net force on a car in the $y$ direction is non-zero because there is vertical motion.
The wings of a jet plane must be banked at an angle $\theta$ to fly along a circular path.
Upward thrust acts normally to an aeroplane body.
Newton's first law explains why an object moves in a straight line in the absence of a force.
Centripetal force is the force acting towards the centre of the circular path.
Centrifugal force is a real force acting on an object along the string.
Centrifugal force is an apparent or fictitious force equal and opposite to the centripetal force.
The centrifugal force is caused by the effect of inertia.
In a laboratory centrifuge, denser particles rise to the top of the tube.
The sedimentation principle states that radial acceleration causes denser particles to settle to the bottom.
A centrifugal pump converts mechanical energy into hydraulic energy.
Hydraulic energy is also known as pressure energy.
The centrifugal action of a washing machine is used to wash the clothes with soap.
Centrifugal governors consist of two balls of equal mass attached to arms.
The centrifugal governor is used to measure and regulate the speed of a machine.
A satellite orbiting a planet is supported by the centrifugal force.
Removing excess water by spin drying clothes is done with the help of centrifugal force.
Centrifugal force results from the effect of inertia.
Centripetal force is directed towards the axis of rotation or centre of curvature.
